summary: PR #2316 added substantial grfzerocar decompilation work in src/melee/gr/grfzerocar.c, including F-Zero car data structs, transform defaults, a 30-entry child table, setup_car_child, and grFZeroCar_801CAFBC. It also exposed grAnime_801C6C0C by removing static in granime.c and adding a header prototype so grfzerocar.c could call it. Bot report evidence shows grfzerocar .rodata became a new match and grFZeroCar_801CAFBC improved to 98.74%, while related data sections improved but were not fully matched.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation addition for grfzerocar; adds structs, constants, data table, helper setup_car_child, and grFZeroCar_801CAFBC implementation.
Changed grAnime_801C6C0C from static to externally visible so it can be called from grfzerocar.c.

what_changed.text: Added includes for grfzerocar, granime, grdatfiles, ground, ground types, lb_00F9, mtx, aobj, jobj, and object support in grfzerocar.c.
Added grFZeroCarEntry with two s16 fields and four child indices, plus grFZeroCar_RST with scale, translate, and rotate fields.
Added grFZeroCar_803B7E50 as default scale/translate/rotation data and grFZeroCar_803E0BD8 as a 30-entry child-index table.

Added static inline setup_car_child, which loads an archive and jobj by ext_count, reads animation/material pointers from archive data using an offset, calls grAnime_801C6C0C, requests animation, sets AObj rate through HSD_ForeachAnim, attaches the child, and scales the parent X/Y.

Added grFZeroCar_801CAFBC, which sets root scale, optionally calls lb_8000F9F8, allocates transform JObjs, iterates 30 table entries, applies default translate/rotation, scales by Ground_801C0498, resolves up to four child JObjs via Ground_801C4100, and invokes setup_car_child for each resolved child.
Changed grAnime_801C6C0C from file-static to externally visible and added its prototype to granime.h.

Bot report recorded one new match for main/melee/gr/grfzerocar .rodata (+40 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%) and improvements for grFZeroCar_801CAFBC (+2977 bytes, 0.00% to 98.74%), .data (+388 bytes, 99.10%), .sdata2 (+14 bytes, 46.15%), and .sdata (+9 bytes, 29.63%).

smart_moves.text: The PR used an actual header export for grAnime_801C6C0C instead of a source-local declaration in grfzerocar.c.
The large repeated car setup was expressed as a 30-iteration loop over a table rather than fully repeated generated blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 30; i++, data_ptr++, tbl++)

smart_moves.text: Repeated child setup was factored into setup_car_child, keeping animation attachment and scale operations in one helper.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: repeated calls to setup_car_child(c0/c1/c2/c3, ext_count, offset, scale_factor)

smart_moves.text: Raw assert expansions were represented with HSD_ASSERT at recovered source lines.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a newly decompiled translation unit needs an existing helper from a sibling file, expose the helper through the owning header rather than adding a local declaration.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grAnime_801C6C0C changed from static to non-static
raw_diff: grAnime_801C6C0C prototype added to granime.h

decomp_lessons.lesson: For repeated object construction driven by compact child-index data, a natural table loop plus a small setup helper can capture a large amount of code progress cleanly.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report grFZeroCar_801CAFBC improved to 98.74%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Preserve uncertainty for partially typed archive payloads: this PR still uses byte-pointer offset reads into archive data, so the recovered structure was not fully known from the slice.

naming_conventions.text: New grfzerocar symbols use the file prefix grFZeroCar_ for address-style globals/functions and grFZeroCarEntry/grFZeroCar_RST for local recovered structs.
Several local names are role-oriented where supported by use, including root, child, tra_jobj, ground_scale, ext_count, offset, data_ptr, tbl, scale_factor, and c0/c1/c2/c3.
Unknown struct fields remain conservative as unk0, unk2, and children; archive internals still use unk4 and unk8 from the existing type.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: A narrow PAD_STACK(0x18) was added in grFZeroCar_801CAFBC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The slice does not include author justification or objdiff details specifically proving PAD_STACK necessity; only the bot progress report is available.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: The implementation uses local Vec3 and Quaternion copies before HSD_JObjSetScale, HSD_JObjSetTranslate, and HSD_JObjSetRotation calls, which may reflect stack/codegen shaping for these APIs.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Vec3 scl_local/trans_local and Quaternion rot_local
raw_diff: assignments from grFZeroCar_803B7E50 before setter calls
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: No verifier explanation in the PR slice confirms why this exact local-copy shape was needed.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: The 30-entry table drives both child selection and the active-child count used to choose scale_factor values 1.1, 1.0, or 0.9.
The PR keeps some raw archive offset reads for animation and material pointers, likely because a more precise archive entry type was not recovered in this slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is a potential future cleanup area under typed-fields-over-pointer-math, not an accepted style conclusion.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Export existing sibling helpers through the owning header when a new TU needs them
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2316 made grAnime_801C6C0C non-static in granime.c and added its prototype to granime.h so grfzerocar.c could call it, instead of adding a local declaration.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grfzerocar.c includes gr/granime.h and calls grAnime_801C6C0C

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use data-driven loops and small helpers for repeated JObj child setup
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2316 expressed F-Zero car child setup as a 30-entry table loop and factored repeated animation/attachment work into setup_car_child, contributing to a reported 98.74% improvement for grFZeroCar_801CAFBC.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 30; i++, data_ptr++, tbl++)
text_corpus: bot report grFZeroCar_801CAFBC +2977 bytes to 98.74%

source_update_candidates.title: Document AI-assisted large-function PRs as needing explicit validation notes
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2316 author stated the large function was produced with Claude assistance, but the loaded slice contains only a bot progress report and no detailed validation or cleanup notes. Curator may consider whether standards should ask AI-assisted decomp PRs to record narrow objdiff/build evidence and known remaining nonmatches.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: "largest function I've sicced Claude on"
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a possible process standard candidate, not an accepted fact; the PR slice shows AI assistance and limited validation evidence but no review discussion.

source_update_candidates.title: grfzerocar depends on granime helper grAnime_801C6C0C
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2316 added a grfzerocar.c call to grAnime_801C6C0C and exposed that helper through granime.h.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-level dependency fact for curator review.

rejection_notes: Do not record grFZeroCar_801CAFBC as fully matched; bot report says 98.74%, not 100%.
Do not treat PAD_STACK(0x18) as a generally accepted tactic from this PR alone; no narrow verifier evidence or explanation was loaded.
Do not infer that Claude output was accepted without manual scrutiny; the PR body only jokes about using Claude, and no review discussion is present.
Do not promote the raw archive pointer arithmetic as preferred style; it remains a potential typed-field recovery area.
